text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Marlena Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Oncologic Surgery / Breast Surgery Service]
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: Fresh, in formalin

---

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:

  A: LEFT BREAST, LUMPECTOMY WITH WIRE LOCALIZATION (CLIP-TARGETED RESECTION)
  B: SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #1, LEFT AXILLA (LABELED INTRAOPERATIVELY)
  C: SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #2, LEFT AXILLA
  D: SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #3, LEFT AXILLA
  E: LEFT AXILLARY CONTENTS, LEVELS I AND II (AXILLARY LYMPH NODE DISSECTION)

---

CLINICAL HISTORY:
A 38-year-old premenopausal woman with a known diagnosis of stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, upper outer quadrant. The patient received neoadjuvant chemotherapy consisting of dose-dense doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide (ddAC) x 4 cycles followed by weekly paclitaxel x 12 doses, with concurrent pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-522 schema). Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant confirmed. Pre-treatment tumor size 3.1 cm with biopsy-proven axillary lymph node involvement. Mid-treatment MRI demonstrated partial response (residual mass 1.8 cm). Patient now presents for definitive surgery: left partial mastectomy (lumpectomy) with wire localization and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Intraoperative frozen section of SLN #1 showed micrometastatic involvement, prompting completion axillary lymph node dissection (levels I-II). Tissue marker clip placed at time of initial biopsy was confirmed present in specimen by intraoperative radiography.

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY:

Specimen A (Lumpectomy):
Received fresh, subsequently inked with six-color margin inking per institutional protocol: anterior (blue), posterior (black), superior (green), inferior (yellow), medial (red), lateral (orange). The specimen measures 4.5 cm (mediolateral) x 3.8 cm (superoinferior) x 2.2 cm (anteroposterior) and weighs 24 grams. The outer surface is irregular with a lobulated contour and attached fibroadipose tissue. A metallic biopsy clip is identified on specimen radiograph in the central area. On serial sectioning, a firm, stellate, ill-defined area of fibrosis and pallor is identified in the upper outer aspect of the specimen, measuring approximately 2.0 x 1.8 x 1.5 cm. Within this region, a small focus of firmer, tan-white, slightly granular tissue is present, estimated at approximately 1.5 cm, distinct from the surrounding fibrotic stroma. No grossly necrotic areas or hemorrhagic foci identified. No satellite nodules visible. The margins appear grossly uninvolved; the closest margin by gross inspection is posterior (estimated 4 mm). Sections are submitted as follows: A1 -- closest posterior margin with fibrotic mass (3 sections); A2-A4 -- fibrotic mass/tumor bed, perpendicular and parallel sections (6 sections); A5 -- anterior margin; A6 -- superior margin; A7 -- inferior margin; A8 -- medial margin; A9 -- lateral margin; A10 -- uninvolved breast parenchyma.

Specimen B (Sentinel Lymph Node #1):
A single lymph node, 1.3 x 1.0 x 0.8 cm, with a firm, tan-white cut surface. The hilum is identifiable. A focal firm white nodule, approximately 0.4 mm, is appreciated on close sectioning. Submitted entirely in two cassettes (B1-B2).

Specimen C (Sentinel Lymph Node #2):
A single lymph node, 0.9 x 0.7 x 0.5 cm, soft, with a tan homogeneous cut surface and preserved fatty hilum. Submitted entirely (C1).

Specimen D (Sentinel Lymph Node #3):
A single lymph node, 0.8 x 0.6 x 0.5 cm, soft, with a tan homogeneous cut surface. Submitted entirely (D1).

Specimen E (Axillary Lymph Node Dissection, Levels I-II):
Received as a fibroadipose tissue specimen measuring 7.5 x 5.5 x 2.5 cm and weighing 36 grams. On careful dissection, 14 lymph nodes are identified ranging from 0.3 to 1.5 cm in greatest dimension. The largest node (1.5 cm) has a partially effaced, tan-white cut surface but no gross extranodal extension identified. All 14 nodes are submitted (E1-E7, 2 nodes per cassette).

---

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:

Specimen A -- Left Breast Lumpectomy:
Sections show residual invasive ductal carcinoma, histologic grade 3 (Nottingham grading system, score 7/9: tubule formation 3, nuclear pleomorphism 3, mitotic rate 1; note mitotic rate has decreased compared to the pre-treatment biopsy where the Nottingham score was 9/9, consistent with treatment effect on proliferative activity). The residual carcinoma is present as irregular nests and single cells embedded within an extensively fibrotic and hyalinized stroma. The residual tumor focus measures 1.4 cm in greatest dimension.

Treatment effect is prominent and extensive: the tumor bed demonstrates large zones of paucicellular hyalinized fibrosis with foamy macrophage infiltrate, hemosiderin deposition, and scattered ghost cell formations (acellular keratin outlines consistent with prior viable tumor). Residual viable carcinoma comprises approximately 45% of the original tumor bed area, with approximately 55% of the bed occupied by treatment-related fibrotic/necrotic change. No comedonecrosis. No in situ carcinoma component identified in the residual tumor.

Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified in the residual specimen (note: lymphovascular invasion was present on the pre-treatment core needle biopsy; its absence in the post-treatment specimen is consistent with treatment-related change).

Microcalcifications: Present within areas of fibrosis; not associated with residual carcinoma.

Surrounding breast parenchyma: Shows radiation-type stromal changes (note: patient has not yet received radiation at time of surgery -- likely changes represent ischemic/reactive changes from neoadjuvant therapy). No atypical ductal hyperplasia. No lobular neoplasia. No additional foci of malignancy.

Stromal tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): Assessed in residual viable tumor area per international TIL working group recommendations. TILs estimated at approximately 10% (markedly decreased from 40% on pre-treatment biopsy; this reduction is consistent with a treatment-induced immunological response and immune cell depletion within the tumor microenvironment).

Surgical Margins:
  - Anterior: Negative; distance to carcinoma > 10 mm
  - Posterior: Negative; closest margin -- carcinoma present 3 mm from inked posterior margin (focally approached by single carcinoma cells with surrounding desmoplastic stroma)
  - Superior: Negative; distance > 8 mm
  - Inferior: Negative; distance > 7 mm
  - Medial: Negative; distance > 6 mm
  - Lateral: Negative; distance > 10 mm
  All margins are negative for invasive carcinoma per SSO-ASTRO guideline criteria (no ink on tumor).

Specimen B -- Sentinel Lymph Node #1:
Sections show a lymph node with reactive follicular hyperplasia and partial sinus histiocytosis consistent with post-treatment changes. On examination of step sections (3 levels, H&E plus immunohistochemical stain for pancytokeratin AE1/AE3), a single focus of isolated tumor cells/micrometastasis is identified measuring 0.4 mm in greatest dimension, present within the subcapsular sinus. The focus consists of a small cluster of cytologically malignant epithelioid cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio and moderate eosinophilic cytoplasm, highlighted by AE1/AE3 immunostain. No associated desmoplastic response. Extranodal extension: not identified. Classification: isolated tumor cells (ITC) / micrometastasis (pN0(i+) per AJCC 8th edition; cluster < 0.2 mm criteria for ITC met; pathologist concurs with ITC/micrometastasis borderline classification at 0.4 mm -- classified as pN0(i+) per institutional protocol given isolated single-focus cluster without desmoplastic response).

Specimen C -- Sentinel Lymph Node #2:
Sections show a benign lymph node with reactive changes and sinus histiocytosis. No evidence of metastatic carcinoma on H&E or cytokeratin immunostain.

Specimen D -- Sentinel Lymph Node #3:
Sections show a benign lymph node, no evidence of metastatic carcinoma.

Specimen E -- Axillary Lymph Node Dissection (Levels I-II):
Fourteen lymph nodes are identified and examined in their entirety. All 14 lymph nodes are negative for metastatic carcinoma. The largest node (1.5 cm) shows reactive follicular hyperplasia with sinus histiocytosis and prominent plasma cell infiltration, consistent with post-treatment reactive changes. No extranodal extension. No evidence of metastatic carcinoma in any of the 14 axillary nodes.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES / IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

Performed on sections of residual invasive carcinoma (Specimen A, block A2):

  Estrogen Receptor (ER) [clone SP1, Ventana]:
    RESULT: NEGATIVE
    Percent positive nuclei: 0%
    Staining intensity: 0 (absent)
    Allred score: 0/8
    Internal positive control: present and intact

  Progesterone Receptor (PR) [clone 1E2, Ventana]:
    RESULT: NEGATIVE
    Percent positive nuclei: 0%
    Staining intensity: 0 (absent)
    Allred score: 0/8
    Internal positive control: present and intact

  HER2/neu [clone 4B5, Ventana]:
    RESULT: NEGATIVE (1+)
    Staining pattern: faint/incomplete membrane staining in < 10% of tumor cells
    Score: 1+ (negative per ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines)
    FISH: Not performed (IHC 1+ does not require reflex FISH per current guidelines)

  Ki-67 [clone 30-9, Ventana]:
    RESULT: Approximately 60% of residual viable tumor nuclei positive
    Note: Represents a decrease from the pre-treatment biopsy Ki-67 of 85%, consistent with partial response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy

  Pancytokeratin AE1/AE3 [Dako]:
    Performed on sentinel lymph node step sections
    Confirms isolated tumor cell cluster in SLN #1 (0.4 mm focus)
    Negative in SLN #2, SLN #3, and all 14 ALND nodes

  PD-L1 (CPS, 22C3 assay, Dako Agilent): Pending at time of final sign-out; to be reported as addendum.

  Note: PD-L1 was 15 (CPS, positive) on pre-treatment biopsy. Current result pending.

---

GRADING OF TREATMENT RESPONSE:

  Miller-Payne Grade: 3
  (Definition: 30-90% reduction in tumor cellularity compared to pre-treatment biopsy; pre-treatment Ki-67 85% with solid nests and central necrosis; post-treatment: scattered irregular nests and single cells within fibrotic stroma, Ki-67 60%, estimated 45% residual cellularity)

  Residual Cancer Burden (RCB) Calculation [MD Anderson methodology]:
    Primary tumor bed dimensions: 2.0 x 1.8 cm (longest cross-sectional dimensions of tumor bed)
    Proportion of tumor bed with invasive carcinoma: 0.45 (45%)
    Residual carcinoma cellularity: 45%
    In situ component proportion: 0
    Lymph node involvement: 1 positive node (largest metastasis 0.4 mm)
    RCB index (calculated): approximately 2.1
    RCB Class: RCB-II (intermediate residual disease)
    (Note: RCB-0 = pCR; RCB-I = minimal residual disease; RCB-II = intermediate; RCB-III = extensive residual disease)

---

FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:

A. LEFT BREAST, LUMPECTOMY:
   - Residual INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA, grade 3 (Nottingham score 7/9), measuring 1.4 cm in greatest dimension, with extensive treatment-related changes (approximately 55% fibrosis/treatment effect in tumor bed)
   - Estrogen receptor: NEGATIVE (0%, Allred 0/8)
   - Progesterone receptor: NEGATIVE (0%, Allred 0/8)
   - HER2: NEGATIVE (IHC 1+)
   - Ki-67: 60% (residual tumor)
   - Stromal TILs: 10% (residual tumor)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - All surgical margins NEGATIVE (closest: posterior margin, 3 mm)
   - Miller-Payne grade: 3
   - RCB class: RCB-II

B. SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #1, LEFT AXILLA:
   - ONE (1) LYMPH NODE WITH ISOLATED TUMOR CELLS / MICROMETASTASIS (0.4 mm cluster, subcapsular sinus; AE1/AE3 positive)
   - Classified as: pN0(i+) per AJCC 8th edition
   - No extranodal extension

C. SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #2, LEFT AXILLA:
   - ONE (1) BENIGN LYMPH NODE, NO EVIDENCE OF METASTATIC CARCINOMA (0/1)

D. SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #3, LEFT AXILLA:
   - ONE (1) BENIGN LYMPH NODE, NO EVIDENCE OF METASTATIC CARCINOMA (0/1)

E. LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE DISSECTION, LEVELS I-II:
   - FOURTEEN (14) BENIGN LYMPH NODES, NO EVIDENCE OF METASTATIC CARCINOMA (0/14)
   - Reactive changes consistent with post-treatment effect

---

SUMMARY LYMPH NODE STATUS:
   Total nodes examined: 17 (3 sentinel + 14 ALND)
   Positive nodes: 1 of 17 (SLN #1, ITC/micrometastasis, 0.4 mm)
   ALND nodes: 0 of 14 positive

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, post-neoadjuvant therapy):
   ypT1c (residual invasive carcinoma 1.4 cm)
   ypN0(i+) (isolated tumor cells only; single focus 0.4 mm in 1 of 17 nodes)
   cM0
   Overall Stage: ypT1c ypN0(i+) M0

   Note on staging: The designation ypN0(i+) reflects the presence of isolated tumor cells (cluster <= 0.2 mm, or in this borderline case, a single micrometastatic cluster of 0.4 mm without desmoplastic response or lymphovascular efferent invasion, classified as ITC per institutional pathology consensus). The clinical significance of residual ITC/micrometastasis after neoadjuvant therapy in the context of TNBC is under active investigation.

---

ADDENDUM (if applicable): PD-L1 CPS (22C3) result to be reported as addendum upon availability.

Signing Pathologist: [Surgical Pathology Attending, Breast Subspecialty Division]
Case reviewed by: [Breast Pathology Fellow]
Specimen adequacy confirmed.
Report finalized.
